description: Emotion-labeled Nepali sentences for TTS emotional prosody evaluation (NepTTS-Bench), testing whether synthesized speech conveys appropriate emotional tone, pitch, and rhythm

notes.usage: Reference data for evaluating TTS emotional expressiveness. Each sentence has a clear emotional valence that should be reflected in prosody, pitch contour, tempo, and voice quality.
notes.benchmark_inspiration: Modeled after EmergentTTS-Eval and InstructTTSEval approaches to emotion prosody testing
notes.evaluation: A good TTS system should produce speech where the emotion is identifiable by listeners without seeing the text label
notes.intensity_levels: mild = subtle emotional coloring, moderate = clearly emotional, strong = intense/heightened emotion
notes.coverage: Covers 8 emotion categories: happy, sad, angry, fearful, surprised, calm, urgent, sympathetic

happy.prosodic_features: higher pitch, wider pitch range, faster tempo, bright voice quality
happy.context: Reacting to unexpectedly good news from a friend or family member
happy.description: Exclamatory expression of delight with the interjection 'waah' signaling strong positive emotion

happy.prosodic_features: elevated pitch throughout, energetic tempo, emphatic stress on 'dherai' and 'khusi'
happy.context: Expressing personal joy about a good day or positive event
happy.description: Direct declaration of happiness with intensifier 'dherai' amplifying the emotion

happy.english: We won, I can't even believe it!
happy.prosodic_features: high pitch onset, rising-falling contour on 'jityaũ', accelerated tempo, breathy excitement
happy.context: Celebrating a team victory in sports or competition
happy.description: Jubilant exclamation combining triumph with disbelief; typical of sports celebrations

happy.sentence: आज मेरो मन फुलेको फूल जस्तो छ।
happy.romanization: aaja mero man phuleko phool jasto chha.
happy.english: Today my heart feels like a blooming flower.
happy.prosodic_features: warm tone, gentle rising pitch, moderate tempo with slight lengthening on 'phuleko phool'
happy.context: Sharing a feeling of contentment and inner joy with someone close
happy.description: Poetic expression of happiness using a common Nepali metaphor of a blooming flower

happy.sentence: यो सुनेर मलाई कत्ति खुसी लाग्यो!
happy.romanization: yo sunera malai katti khusi laagyo!
happy.english: Hearing this made me so happy!
happy.prosodic_features: rising pitch on 'katti khusi', upbeat tempo, warm vocal quality
happy.context: Responding positively to someone's good news or achievement
happy.description: Reactive happiness triggered by hearing something pleasant; natural conversational expression

happy.sentence: बधाई छ! तपाईंले त कमाल गर्नुभयो!
happy.romanization: badhaai chha! tapaaĩle ta kamaal garnubhayo!
happy.prosodic_features: high pitch peak on 'badhaai', emphatic stress on 'kamaal', enthusiastic tempo
happy.context: Congratulating someone on a major achievement like passing an exam or getting a promotion
happy.description: Celebratory congratulation with emphatic praise; uses formal honorific verb form

happy.sentence: चाड आउँदैछ, कत्ति रमाइलो हुन्छ होला!
happy.romanization: chaad aaũdaichha, katti ramailo hunchha holaa!
happy.english: The festival is coming, it will be so much fun!
happy.prosodic_features: gradually rising pitch, anticipatory intonation, lively tempo
happy.context: Looking forward to an upcoming festival like Dashain or Tihar
happy.description: Anticipatory excitement about a Nepali festival; reflects cultural celebration context

happy.english: The best day ever so far!
happy.prosodic_features: wide pitch range, emphatic stress on 'sabai bhandaa ramro', fast enthusiastic tempo
happy.context: Exclaiming about having an exceptionally good day
happy.description: Superlative expression of joy; the compound 'ahilesammkai' emphasizes the unprecedented nature

sad.english: They are no longer among us.
sad.prosodic_features: low pitch, slow tempo, falling intonation, heavy pauses between phrases
sad.context: Speaking about someone who has passed away, at a funeral or memorial
sad.description: Solemn expression of loss using the respectful 'uhaa~' and formal negative verb; deeply emotional in Nepali culture

sad.prosodic_features: low pitch, very slow tempo, breathy voice quality, prolonged vowels on 'dherai'
sad.context: Expressing deep emotional pain after a personal loss or betrayal
sad.description: Direct expression of heartache; 'man dukheko' is the standard Nepali idiom for emotional pain

sad.english: This farewell has been very difficult.
sad.prosodic_features: low-mid pitch, slow tempo, slight voice tremor, falling contour on 'gaahro bhayo'
sad.context: Saying goodbye to someone leaving for a long time, such as a family member going abroad
sad.description: Expression of sadness at parting; common in Nepali context of labor migration

sad.sentence: कहिलेकाहीँ एक्लो हुँदा रुन मन लाग्छ।
sad.romanization: kahilekahĩ eklo hũdaa runa man laagchha.
sad.english: Sometimes when I'm alone, I feel like crying.
sad.prosodic_features: soft voice, low pitch, slow deliberate pacing, slight breathiness
sad.context: Confiding in someone about feelings of loneliness
sad.description: Vulnerable admission of loneliness and sadness; intimate conversational register

sad.english: It feels like everyone has forgotten.
sad.prosodic_features: flat low pitch, slow tempo, resigned falling intonation
sad.context: Feeling neglected or forgotten by friends and family
sad.description: Expression of neglect and isolation; the 'jasto laagchha' construction conveys subjective feeling

sad.english: When I remember them, my eyes get wet.
sad.prosodic_features: low pitch, gentle slow tempo, slight voice cracking quality, falling contour
sad.context: Remembering a deceased loved one or someone who has gone far away
sad.description: Poignant expression of missing someone; 'aakhaa bhijinchha' is an idiomatic way to describe tearing up

sad.english: Life sometimes gives a lot of sorrow.
sad.prosodic_features: low pitch, measured slow tempo, philosophical resigned tone
sad.context: Reflecting on hardships in a philosophical conversation
sad.description: Reflective, philosophical sadness about life's difficulties; mild intensity suggests acceptance

sad.sentence: मैले धेरै कोसिसँ गरेँ, तर हुन सकेन।
sad.romanization: maile dherai kosis garẽ, tara huna sakena.
sad.english: I tried very hard, but it could not happen.
sad.prosodic_features: rising pitch on effort clause, sharp fall on 'tara huna sakena', slow defeated tempo
sad.context: Expressing disappointment after a failed attempt at something important
sad.description: Disappointment after failure despite effort; the contrast between trying and failing creates emotional tension

angry.prosodic_features: sharp rising pitch on 'kasari', increased loudness, clipped articulation, fast tempo
angry.context: Reacting to an unjust decision or unfair treatment
angry.description: Rhetorical question expressing indignation; the formal 'swikaarya' adds gravity

angry.english: I don't like this kind of behavior!
angry.prosodic_features: high pitch, loud volume, emphatic stress on 'yasto' and 'pardaina', fast forceful tempo
angry.context: Confronting someone about their rude or disrespectful behavior
angry.description: Direct expression of displeasure and anger at mistreatment

angry.prosodic_features: high pitch throughout, emphatic stress on 'kasaile' and 'lĩdaina', aggressive tempo
angry.context: Frustrated at a group or organization where things keep going wrong
angry.description: Frustrated accusation of collective irresponsibility; common in workplace or public discourse

angry.english: Enough, I can't tolerate this anymore!
angry.prosodic_features: abrupt onset on 'bas', rising intensity, fast tempo, tense voice quality
angry.context: Reaching a breaking point after repeated provocations or injustice
angry.description: Expression of having reached one's limit; 'bas' signals finality and the breaking point

angry.english: How long will this corruption go on?
angry.prosodic_features: sharp pitch peaks, indignant tone, measured but forceful tempo
angry.context: Expressing anger about systemic corruption in public institutions
angry.description: Rhetorical question expressing societal anger; reflects common Nepali public discourse

angry.prosodic_features: sharp accusatory tone, high pitch on 'laaj', pointed emphasis, fast clipped delivery
angry.context: Confronting someone directly about shameful behavior
angry.description: Accusatory question invoking shame; uses informal 'timi' register to show disrespect in anger

angry.sentence: मैले कति पटक भनेँ, तर कसैले सुन्दैन।
angry.romanization: maile kati patak bhanẽ, tara kasaile sundaina.
angry.english: How many times have I said it, but nobody listens.
angry.prosodic_features: exasperated rising-falling pattern, stress on 'kati patak', frustrated slow-then-fast tempo
angry.context: Frustrated at being repeatedly ignored or having advice disregarded
angry.description: Expression of exasperation at being unheard; the repetition element adds to the frustration

fearful.prosodic_features: uncertain rising intonation, slightly trembling voice, slow tentative tempo
fearful.context: Worrying about an uncertain future outcome, such as exam results or medical tests
fearful.description: Anxious uncertainty expressed through the speculative particle 'holaa'

fearful.prosodic_features: low pitch, slow tempo, tight voice quality, emphasis on 'dherai chintit'
fearful.context: Expressing worry about a family member's health or a serious situation
fearful.description: Direct statement of anxiety; 'chintit' is the standard Nepali word for worried/anxious

fearful.sentence: बाहिर निकै अन्धेरो छ, मलाई डर लागिरहेको छ।
fearful.romanization: baahira nikai andhero chha, malai dar laagirakheko chha.
fearful.english: It's very dark outside, I am feeling scared.
fearful.prosodic_features: low hushed pitch, slow cautious tempo, breathy voice, rising anxiety in second clause
fearful.context: Feeling afraid at night in an unfamiliar or dark place
fearful.description: Fear of darkness with ongoing state expressed by continuous tense 'laagirakheko chha'

fearful.english: Earthquake! Quick, hide under the table!
fearful.prosodic_features: sharp sudden onset, high-pitched alarm, very fast urgent tempo, loud volume
fearful.context: Reacting to an earthquake, shouting instructions in panic
fearful.description: Panicked response to a natural disaster; combines fear with urgent command; highly relevant in earthquake-prone Nepal

fearful.sentence: यो बाटो सुरक्षित छैन, अर्को बाटो जाऔँ।
fearful.romanization: yo baato surakshit chhaina, arko baato jaaũ.
fearful.english: This road isn't safe, let's take another road.
fearful.prosodic_features: cautious low tone, deliberate pacing, slight urgency in second clause
fearful.context: Warning companions about a dangerous path during travel in hilly terrain
fearful.description: Cautious fear expressed as a practical suggestion; common in Nepal's mountainous geography

fearful.sentence: मलाई यो ठाउँमा एक्लै बस्न डर लाग्छ।
fearful.romanization: malai yo thaaũmaa eklai basna dar laagchha.
fearful.english: I'm afraid to stay alone in this place.
fearful.prosodic_features: low soft pitch, slow measured tempo, slightly tremulous voice quality
fearful.context: Expressing discomfort about staying alone in an unfamiliar or eerie location
fearful.description: Admission of fear about solitude in a specific place; 'dar laagchha' is the standard fear expression

surprised.prosodic_features: sharp high pitch onset on 'are', wide pitch jump, abrupt tempo change
surprised.context: Unexpectedly running into someone in an unlikely place
surprised.description: Classic surprise interjection 'are' followed by disbelief; informal register

surprised.prosodic_features: high pitch, wide range, emphatic stress on 'vishwaas nai', fast excited tempo
surprised.context: Reacting to something completely unexpected, positive or negative
surprised.description: Expression of total disbelief; the emphatic particle 'nai' intensifies the incredulity

surprised.sentence: ल, यो त मैले सोचेकै थिइनँ!
surprised.romanization: la, yo ta maile sochekei thiinã!
surprised.english: Well, I had never even thought of this!
surprised.prosodic_features: rising pitch with high peak on 'sochekei', moderate tempo with pause after 'la'
surprised.context: Discovering something completely unexpected that changes one's perspective
surprised.description: Surprise at something unforeseen; 'la' as a discourse starter signals processing of new information

surprised.english: Amazing! How did it happen so fast?
surprised.prosodic_features: high pitch exclamation, quick tempo, rising question intonation on 'kasari bhayo'
surprised.context: Surprised by the speed of something being completed
surprised.description: Wonder and surprise at unexpected quickness; 'achamma' is a standard surprise exclamation

surprised.sentence: ए, तिमीले त पुरस्कार जित्यौ रे!
surprised.romanization: e, timile ta puraskaar jityau re!
surprised.english: Hey, I heard you won an award!
surprised.prosodic_features: high onset on 'e', rising surprised pitch, emphasis on 'puraskaar', reportative 're' with trailing intonation
surprised.context: Learning about someone's unexpected achievement through hearsay
surprised.description: Surprised reaction to hearsay news; the reportative particle 're' indicates second-hand information

surprised.english: This turned out to be completely different!
surprised.prosodic_features: wide pitch excursion, stress on 'purai pharak', mirative intonation on 'rahechha'
surprised.context: Discovering something is entirely different from expectations
surprised.description: Mirative surprise using 'rahechha' which conveys newly discovered information in Nepali

calm.prosodic_features: flat even pitch, moderate steady tempo, neutral voice quality, simple falling terminal
calm.context: Stating a fact in everyday conversation or in response to a question
calm.description: Simple factual statement with no emotional charge; baseline neutral prosody

calm.english: The meeting will be at three o'clock.
calm.prosodic_features: level pitch, steady moderate tempo, clear articulation, neutral falling intonation
calm.context: Informing colleagues about a scheduled meeting time
calm.description: Informational statement about a routine event; standard workplace communication

calm.english: This book is good to read.
calm.prosodic_features: moderate even pitch, relaxed tempo, gentle falling contour
calm.context: Making a calm recommendation about a book during conversation
calm.description: Mild positive assessment delivered in a matter-of-fact tone; no strong emotional engagement

calm.english: Yesterday I went to the market and bought vegetables.
calm.prosodic_features: flat pitch, steady narrative tempo, simple declarative intonation
calm.context: Casually describing a routine daily activity
calm.description: Neutral narrative about daily life; standard conversational register without emotional weight

calm.english: The capital of Nepal is Kathmandu.
calm.prosodic_features: level informational pitch, clear even tempo, textbook-style falling terminal
calm.context: Stating a geographical fact in a classroom or informational setting
calm.description: Pure factual statement; useful as a prosodic baseline for comparison with emotional sentences

urgent.english: Take them to the hospital quickly!
urgent.prosodic_features: high pitch, very fast tempo, loud volume, sharp emphatic stress on 'chhito'
urgent.context: Medical emergency where someone needs immediate hospital care
urgent.description: Emergency command; the informal imperative 'laijau' signals urgency over politeness

urgent.prosodic_features: very high pitch, maximum tempo, commanding loud volume, sharp onset on 'turuntai'
urgent.context: Emergency evacuation during a fire, earthquake, or other disaster
urgent.description: Urgent evacuation command using formal imperative; urgency overrides the politeness of '-nuhoss'

urgent.english: Close the door, someone is coming!
urgent.prosodic_features: fast tempo, high pitch, hushed intensity, quick pause between clauses
urgent.context: Warning someone to close the door quickly due to a perceived threat
urgent.description: Urgent warning with immediate action command; informal register showing closeness and urgency

urgent.english: There's no time, we must go right now!
urgent.prosodic_features: fast compressed tempo, high pitch, emphatic 'nai', stressed obligation in 'jaanuparchha'
urgent.context: Rushing to catch a bus, plane, or meet a critical deadline
urgent.description: Time-pressure urgency; the combination of negation and necessity creates strong imperative force

urgent.sentence: एम्बुलेन्स बोलाउनुहोस्, बिरामी धेरै गम्भीर छ!
urgent.romanization: ambulence bolaunuhoss, biraami dherai gambhir chha!
urgent.english: Call an ambulance, the patient is very serious!
urgent.prosodic_features: high loud pitch, very fast tempo, stress on 'dherai gambhir', commanding tone
urgent.context: Medical emergency requiring immediate professional help
urgent.description: Emergency medical command using formal imperative; the gravity word 'gambhir' signals life-threatening situation

sympathetic.english: Don't grieve, everything will be alright.
sympathetic.prosodic_features: warm low pitch, slow gentle tempo, soothing falling contour, soft voice quality
sympathetic.context: Consoling someone who has experienced a loss or setback
sympathetic.description: Classic consolation phrase; the negative imperative 'namaannuhoss' gently asks to not take sorrow to heart

sympathetic.prosodic_features: low warm pitch, slow tempo, empathetic tone, gentle stress on 'pidaa'
sympathetic.context: Expressing empathy to someone going through a difficult time
sympathetic.description: Direct empathetic statement using formal 'tapaaĩ'; 'pidaa' conveys deeper suffering than 'dukha'

sympathetic.sentence: तपाईं एक्लो हुनुहुन्न, हामी सबै साथमा छौँ।
sympathetic.romanization: tapaaĩ eklo hunuhunna, haami sabai saathmaa chhaũ.
sympathetic.english: You are not alone, we are all with you.
sympathetic.prosodic_features: warm reassuring tone, moderate tempo, rising warmth on second clause, gentle emphasis on 'sabai'
sympathetic.context: Reassuring someone who feels isolated during a crisis
sympathetic.description: Reassurance of solidarity; the contrast between 'eklo' and 'sabai saathmaa' creates emotional comfort

sympathetic.english: If you want to cry, cry — you don't have to hold back.
sympathetic.prosodic_features: soft gentle pitch, very slow tempo, permissive warm tone, pause between clauses
sympathetic.context: Giving someone emotional permission to express grief freely
sympathetic.description: Compassionate permission to express emotions; validates grief rather than suppressing it

sympathetic.sentence: समयसँगै यो पीडा कम हुँदै जान्छ।
sympathetic.romanization: samayasãgai yo pidaa kam hũdai jaanchha.
sympathetic.english: With time, this pain will gradually lessen.
sympathetic.prosodic_features: gentle low pitch, slow reassuring tempo, gradual falling contour, soft voice
sympathetic.context: Offering hope to someone dealing with emotional or physical pain
sympathetic.description: Consolation through the promise of healing over time; the progressive 'hũdai jaanchha' conveys gradual change
